perturbation.original_text: Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein; but none of them is responsible for the completeness or accuracy of the information concerning the other persons making the filing, unless such person knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto. None of them is responsible for the completeness or accuracy of the information concerning any person contained therein, including themselves, unless such person knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein; but none of them is responsible for the completeness or accuracy of the information concerning the other persons making the filing', and replacing it with 'None of them is responsible for the completeness or accuracy of the information concerning any person contained therein, including themselves', the agreement now contradicts itself. It initially states each party is responsible for the accuracy of their information, but the modified text suggests no one is responsible for the accuracy of anyone's information, including their own, unless they know it is inaccurate. This introduces uncertainty about liability for inaccurate filings in the initial part of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Exhibit 26 JOINT FILING AGREEMENT Pursuant to and in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and the rules and regulations thereunder (the "Exchange Act") the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing on behalf of each of them of any filing required by such party under Section 13 of the Exchange Act or any rule or regulation thereunder (including any amendment, restatement, supplement, and/or exhibit thereto) with respect to securities of Tallgrass Energy, LP, a Delaware limited partnership, and further agree to the filing, furnishing, and/or incorporation by reference of this Agreement as an exhibit thereto. Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto. None of them is responsible for the completeness or accuracy of the information concerning any person contained therein, including themselves, unless such person knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate. This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto in a signed writing provided to each other party hereto, and then only with respect to such revoking party. This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned hereby execute this Joint Filing Agreement as of February 19, 2020. PRAIRIE ECI ACQUIROR LP By: BIP Holdings Manager L.L.C., its general partner By: /s/ Sean Klimczak Name: Sean Klimczak Title: Senior Managing Director PRAIRIE NON-ECI ACQUIROR LP By: BIP Holdings Manager L.L.C., its general partner By: /s/ Sean Klimczak Name: Sean Klimczak Title: Senior Managing Director PRAIRIE VCOC ACQUIROR LP By: BIP Holdings Manager L.L.C., its general partner By: /s/ Sean Klimczak Name: Sean Klimczak Title: Senior Managing Director PRAIRIE SECONDARY ACQUIROR LP By: BIP Holdings Manager L.L.C., its general partner By: /s/ Sean Klimczak Name: Sean Klimczak Title: Senior Managing Director PRAIRIE SECONDARY ACQUIROR E LP By: BIP Holdings Manager L.L.C., its general partner By: /s/ Sean Klimczak Name: Sean Klimczak Title: Senior Managing Director BIP HOLDINGS MANAGER L.L.C. perturbation.justification: The original text in both instances held parties responsible for the accuracy of their own filings, while the modified text absolves them of this responsibility unless they have knowledge of inaccuracies. This is a direct contradiction: one section mandates accuracy for self-filed information, while the other states there's no such responsibility unless there's known inaccuracy. This creates a clear conflict, likely to cause confusion regarding liability for inaccurate filings.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto in a signed writing provided to each other party hereto, and then only with respect to such revoking party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto with or without signed writing provided to each other party hereto, and then only with respect to such revoking party.
perturbation.explanation: The insertion of 'with or without signed writing' directly contradicts the previous clause which requires signed writing. This amendment creates ambiguity and in-text contradiction regarding the method by which a party can revoke the agreement.

This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto with or without signed writing provided to each other party hereto, and then only with respect to such revoking party. perturbation.justification: The original text states that revocation must be 'in a signed writing'. The changed text allows revocation 'with or without signed writing'. This directly contradicts the original requirement, creating a clear and impactful contradiction. The contradicted text repeats the changed text meaning that the original clause about the signed writing is void due to the updated clause.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts but only the original signed copy shall constitute the definitive instrument.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument' to 'but only the original signed copy shall constitute the definitive instrument' creates a direct contradiction. Initially, each counterpart is equally valid. However, the revised text states only the original is definitive, making the validity of the other copies questionable and creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Exhibit 26 JOINT FILING AGREEMENT Pursuant to and in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and the rules and regulations thereunder (the "Exchange Act") the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing on behalf of each of them of any filing required by such party under Section 13 of the Exchange Act or any rule or regulation thereunder (including any amendment, restatement, supplement, and/or exhibit thereto) with respect to securities of Tallgrass Energy, LP, a Delaware limited partnership, and further agree to the filing, furnishing, and/or incorporation by reference of this Agreement as an exhibit thereto. Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto. None of them is responsible for the completeness or accuracy of the information concerning any person contained therein, including themselves, unless such person knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate. This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto with or without signed writing provided to each other party hereto, and then only with respect to such revoking party. This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts but only the original signed copy shall constitute the definitive instrument. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned hereby execute this Joint Filing Agreement as of February 19, 2020. perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'all counterparts shall constitute one and the same instrument,' implying equal validity. The changed text limits this by saying 'only the original signed copy shall constitute the definitive instrument.' This creates a direct contradiction because the contradicted text explicitly states 'This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts but only the original signed copy shall constitute the definitive instrument.' This contradicts the notion that all counterparts are equally valid, introducing uncertainty and potential unenforceability regarding the copies of the agreement. This falls under Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.
